criteria_met: migration-strategist.md created with 7 reasoning patterns covering expand-contract (Expand-contract discipline), blast radius (Blast radius mapping), dual-write windows (Dual-write/dual-read windows), strangler fig (Strangler fig over big bang), plus phase-gated migration, rollback cost heuristic, migration completion criteria

migration-strategist and complexity-skeptic have distinct, non-overlapping reasoning patterns — zero shared pattern names, minimal vocabulary overlap (migration-strategist focuses on transition execution, complexity-skeptic focuses on evaluation and removal)
All files match harness-engineer.md structure exactly

notes.migration-strategist: Domain Expertise describes HOW the strategist thinks ('transition paths, not target states', 'what are the safe intermediate states'). 7 patterns are actionable migration decision frameworks. Anti-patterns (5 rows) are distinct with clear problem/reason/alternative. Quality Criteria specific (rollback plan per phase, idempotent data migrations).
notes.complexity-skeptic: Domain Expertise describes HOW the skeptic evaluates ('total cost of ownership', 'treats every dependency as a liability until proven otherwise', 'views removal as progress'). 6 patterns are actionable evaluation frameworks. Anti-patterns (5 rows) are distinct. Quality Criteria measurable (documented justification, expiration dates, two-consumer threshold).
notes.cli-designer: Domain Expertise describes HOW the designer thinks about CLI UX ('terminal is a first-class UX surface', 'Unix philosophy'). 7 patterns are actionable design principles. Anti-patterns (5 rows) are distinct CLI-specific issues. Quality Criteria specific (--help, --force, --json, exit codes).
